description: Mid-E01 Prep Exam Question Bank — Verbal Tenses

questions.rationale: Tests 3rd person singular -s rule in Simple Present affirmative. Directly mapped to a card the student has reviewed.

distractor_rationale.a: Base form — correct for I/you/we/they but not he/she/it
distractor_rationale.d: Present participle — no auxiliary, not a valid simple present

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: He does not (doesn't) own a car.
questions.rationale: Tests correct auxiliary for 3rd person singular negative in Simple Present (does not / doesn't).

distractor_rationale.a: don't — correct for I/you/we/they, not he/she/it
distractor_rationale.b: isn't — auxiliary for Present Continuous, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.d: hasn't — auxiliary for Present Perfect, wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.rationale: Tests correct auxiliary for 3rd person singular question in Simple Present.

distractor_rationale.a: Is — auxiliary for Present Continuous questions
distractor_rationale.c: Do — correct for I/you/we/they, not she
distractor_rationale.d: Has — auxiliary for Present Perfect questions

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.card_source: They are building a new house this year.
questions.question_text: They _____ a new house this year.
questions.rationale: Tests the be + verb-ing structure for Present Continuous. The time marker 'this year' signals an ongoing temporary action.

distractor_rationale.a: Simple Present base form — correct for habits, not temporary ongoing actions
distractor_rationale.b: Simple Present 3rd person — wrong for plural subject 'they'
distractor_rationale.d: Present Perfect — no ongoing action indicated here

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: She is studying for her exam.
questions.question_text: "She is studying for her exam." — Which tense is used in this sentence?
questions.rationale: Tests recognition of the is + verb-ing structure. This exact sentence appears on a card the student has reviewed.

distractor_rationale.a: Simple Present uses base form (she studies), not is studying
distractor_rationale.c: Present Perfect uses has/have + past participle (she has studied)
distractor_rationale.d: Simple Past uses the past form (she studied)

questions.instruction_pt: Qual tempo verbal é usado nesta frase?

questions.card_source: She visited her grandmother last week.
questions.rationale: Tests regular verb -ed formation in Simple Past. 'Last week' is the key time marker for Simple Past.

distractor_rationale.a: Base form — Simple Present, not past
distractor_rationale.b: 3rd person Simple Present — wrong tense, also contradicts 'last week'
distractor_rationale.d: Present Perfect — used for unspecified past experience, not 'last week'

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: I did not (didn't) go to the party.
questions.rationale: Tests the did not / didn't auxiliary for Simple Past negatives. Common error is using 'don't' (Simple Present) or 'wasn't' (Past Continuous).

distractor_rationale.a: wasn't — Past Continuous negative auxiliary, wrong structure
distractor_rationale.c: haven't — Present Perfect negative, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.d: don't — Simple Present negative, wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.rationale: Tests the 'Did' auxiliary for Simple Past questions. Note the base verb 'call' (not 'called') after did — a common confusion point.

distractor_rationale.c: Do — Simple Present question auxiliary (wrong tense)

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.rationale: Tests the 'will + base verb' structure for Simple Future. 'Tomorrow' is the key time marker.

distractor_rationale.a: am helping — Present Continuous (can express near future plans, but less standard here)
distractor_rationale.b: help — base form only, no auxiliary, grammatically incomplete as future
distractor_rationale.d: have helped — Present Perfect, indicates completed past action

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.tense: Simple Present / Simple Past / Simple Future
questions.question_text: Which time expression matches the Simple Past tense?
questions.rationale: Tests knowledge of time adverbs as tense markers. 'Yesterday' is the canonical Simple Past marker. Contrasts with the markers for Simple Present (every day), Present Continuous (right now), and Simple Future (tomorrow).

distractor_rationale.a: every day — signals Simple Present (habitual action)
distractor_rationale.b: right now — signals Present Continuous (action in progress)

questions.instruction_pt: Qual expressão de tempo corresponde ao Simple Past?

questions.card_source: I was reading when the phone rang.
questions.rationale: Tests the was/were + verb-ing structure for Past Continuous. The sentence structure (ongoing action interrupted by a shorter past action) is the classic Past Continuous context.

distractor_rationale.a: read — Simple Past, treats both actions as sequential rather than overlapping
distractor_rationale.c: have read — Present Perfect, wrong time reference
distractor_rationale.d: had read — Past Perfect, implies completion before another past event

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.rationale: Tests the negative form of Past Continuous (was/were + not + verb-ing). Each distractor targets a specific common error.

distractor_rationale.a: Uses 'didn't' (Simple Past auxiliary) with -ing form — mixing two structures
distractor_rationale.c: Uses past participle 'drove' instead of present participle 'driving'
distractor_rationale.d: Incorrect word order — negation must come after the auxiliary (wasn't), not before

questions.instruction_pt: Qual frase está correta?

questions.rationale: Tests the have/has + past participle structure for Present Perfect. 'Twice' signals an unspecified life experience — the classic Present Perfect context.

distractor_rationale.a: visited — Simple Past; would require a specific time marker (e.g., 'last year'), not just 'twice'
distractor_rationale.c: was visiting — Past Continuous; implies interruption, not a counted experience
distractor_rationale.d: visit — Simple Present; wrong tense entirely

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: Have you ever climbed a mountain?
questions.rationale: Tests the Present Perfect question auxiliary 'Have'. 'Ever' is a classic Present Perfect question marker. Directly mapped to a card the student reviewed.

distractor_rationale.a: Did — Simple Past auxiliary; would require base verb 'climb' not past participle 'climbed'

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.card_source: She visited her grandmother last week. / I have visited Paris twice.
questions.question_text:
Choose the correct tense for each blank:
"She _____ her grandmother (a) LAST WEEK. She _____ Paris (b) TWICE."
questions.rationale: Tests the core distinction: Simple Past is used with specific time markers ('last week'); Present Perfect is used for unspecified experience ('twice'). This is the most important contrast in the E01 deck.

distractor_rationale.b: Reverses the rule — 'has visited last week' is grammatically incorrect
distractor_rationale.c: All Simple Past — ignores the Present Perfect for unspecified experience
distractor_rationale.d: All Present Perfect — 'has visited last week' is ungrammatical with a specific time marker

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a combinação correta de tempos verbais para as duas frases.

questions.card_source: She will be studying when you call.
questions.rationale: Tests the 'will be + verb-ing' structure for Future Continuous. The sentence expresses an action that will be in progress at a future moment — not just a future event.

distractor_rationale.a: studies — Simple Present, used here only for the subordinate time clause ('when you call'), not the main clause
distractor_rationale.b: will study — Simple Future; correct future tense but misses the ongoing aspect
distractor_rationale.d: is studying — Present Continuous; can express near future but not ongoing-at-a-future-moment

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.rationale: Tests the 'has/have been + verb-ing' structure for Present Perfect Continuous. 'Since 2010' is the canonical marker for an action that started in the past and continues now.

distractor_rationale.a: teaches — Simple Present; ignores the duration starting in the past
distractor_rationale.c: was teaching — Past Continuous; implies the action ended in the past
distractor_rationale.d: had been teaching — Past Perfect Continuous; would require a second past reference point

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: They had left before I arrived.
questions.rationale: Tests the 'had + past participle' structure for Past Perfect. When two past events are in sequence, the earlier one uses Past Perfect ('had left') and the later one uses Simple Past ('arrived').

distractor_rationale.a: left — Simple Past; doesn't show which event happened first
distractor_rationale.b: have left — Present Perfect; refers to present relevance, not a prior past event
distractor_rationale.c: were leaving — Past Continuous; implies ongoing action, not completion before another event

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: She has lived here for five years. / She has been teaching since 2010.
questions.question_text: She has lived here _____ five years.
questions.rationale: Tests the for/since distinction with Present Perfect. 'For' is used with a duration ('five years'); 'since' is used with a starting point ('2010'). Both are heavily represented in the deck.

distractor_rationale.a: since — used with a point in time (e.g., 'since 2010'), not a duration
distractor_rationale.c: ago — used with Simple Past ('five years ago'), not Present Perfect
distractor_rationale.d: during — used with a specific period ('during the meeting'), not a duration of years

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a preposição correta.

questions.question_text:
Read the passage:

"Every morning, she wakes up early. Yesterday, she woke up at 6 AM. Right now, she is having breakfast. She has already made her coffee."

Which sentence uses the Present Perfect?

options.text: She has already made her coffee.

questions.rationale: Tests tense recognition across four different tenses in context: Simple Present, Simple Past, Present Continuous, and Present Perfect. Uses sentences constructed from vocabulary and patterns present in the E01 deck. The marker 'already' is a classic Present Perfect indicator.

distractor_rationale.b: Simple Past — specific completed past event, 'yesterday'
distractor_rationale.c: Present Continuous — action in progress right now, 'right now'

questions.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e identifique qual frase usa o Present Perfect.
